It's fine.
How are you doing?
How's your day?
Okay.

How about you guys?

I feel like across the board, everyone's just, like, so busy, but busy, but good.
So can't complain.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Same here.
Awesome.
Hey, Dan.
Hey, how's it going, everyone?
Hi, Dan.
Hi, Hey.

He's got a few other things going on.
We'll have to do capable hands.
Totally get it.

Yeah.
Happy for him.
Happy for us.
Can you see my screen?

So, we had an increase of 20% in impressions, clicks, engage sessions, and sessions also increased.
We had...

AI mentions, I took a position from the last four weeks, so we were at 30% of presence.
AI, in the same number of prompts we are monitoring.
So if you look at the last four weeks, we were at, we are at 30% presence, and if you look at only the seven last days, we are at 35%.
So like in this, in Scrunches, the tool we use, we can't see a progression, so that's why I take the seven days timeframe and four weeks.
This is SEMrush, pretty much.
Pretty much the same scenery we had by the end of September, but good movement in top three to the first page, right, until position 10.
Okay, so good numbers.

What we're working on right now, we are working based on that concept that you shared, that you don't want to position real-time optimization like the end goal, like you want to be more clear that advisory is also a way to use, a use case for the tool.
So this step-by-step guide to self-optimizing and the name of the plant, actually the name of the plant is the main keyword we're focusing on.
So advisory mode in these articles is communicated as a step.
To a fully self-optimizing plant.
We are also exploring some polymer, the umbrella of polymer.
So polypropylene is one of the article's challenges.
Okay, so this other articles, I just brought some highlights.
Go ahead.
Want to say something?
just said okay.
It's okay.
Okay.
Some clarifications I wanted to do with you.
So we know Imubit is an AI solution that works on top of other tools that produce plant data.
Do you think it's a possibility that we could leverage the keyword related to the names of this platform?
For example, the biggest use case in oil, in the oil and gas industry is with a certain tool.
Do you think we could do that?
for水oke?
it's a orustainability?

So that would be...
That would be...

Like, so we don't just take historian data.
I would say that's probably the most common right now, but that's not the only thing.
So I'm a little, I'm a little hesitant to add that in.
Dan, I don't know if you have any thoughts.
I'm just wondering if there's, if that creates more confusion than it does provide explanation.
Yeah, I'm not sure.
It would be something like, say, for example, five optimizations you can do.
For example, if I have one solution, I have the historian solution in place, but I don't have imubit.
So if I want to create an article about what else, so what are the benefits that this combo would deliver that the historian does not.
Actually, was going to, I had this print from, like this term, historian, appears a lot.
And I was like, this is not the actual term because it's historic, it's not historian.
So I took a look and there's actually a name of a product from Aviva, it's an Emerson, I guess it's a solution from Emerson.
So it's the name of a tool, but also it's, according to ChedPT,
It's used as a term for the whole category as well.
So it's like Gillette.
it's a category tool.
I mean, I'm not sure which Emerson tool you're referring to, but yeah, it's pretty much an industry-wide plant historian is the category.
So it's fine to use historian as a category.
Fine.
Yes.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, that's a pretty well understood and utilized term.
Yeah.
Which brings me to the next topic is about glossary pages.
This is something we actually have in place for other clients.
Okay.
As in just like having additionally to, you have blogs, we have articles.
So having one slash glossary with common common terms.

For example, found one term, it's a sintering.
So sintering is part of a process.
It's like material in small particles, like in bigger particles to be fed into a part of a, so it's part of a system.
For example, the, the kiln or the, the furnace.
So it's part of a system that, that Imubit can optimize, but like we could create like glossary terms to describe these parts of these pieces.
One, the data historian, it's another example.
This could be a glossary term, but not exactly an article.
So for the future, I know you also want to change CMS, so it's probably maybe something not for now.
But just in the future, it's something that we do.
And in some spaces, for the clients we have, there's a lot of competition.
But in your scenario, it's a little bit different.
we have C3 from this example.
have, let me see.
Yeah, I'm not opposed to this idea, but I do think, as you mentioned, it's probably best if we wait until we decide on the CMS, just because the port over, I'd rather just do it fresh.
Are you thinking that each term has its own page, or are you thinking there is a glossary page with all of the terms?
Oh, no, no, no.
Each term has one page, and it wouldn't be like a lot of...
Terms, just the ones that keep coming up frequently, and this is important for us to establish an internal linking structure.
So we keep referring to the same, we would write only about the terms that are more frequent, so a lot of opportunities for internal links there.
Well, one of the, I mean, you have some of the examples here, right?
Like you have the RTO and that kind of thing.
That's one of the things that this industry has a ton of is acronyms, right?
And understanding, APC, RTO, DCS, AP, I mean, just on and on and on.
So where we want to, where we want to consider this and be cognizant is that list could be hundreds.
Yeah, yeah.
Hundreds of terms, right?
And so I'm, I'm thinking about it from the logistics.
side in terms of managing and having, you know, 300 glossary pages on the site and how unwieldy that could become.
I think that we'd have to look at which ones make the most sense, but again, I'm not opposed.
think, I mean, Dan, do you want to weigh in here?
I mean, like, I do like the idea that from, like, an SEO and back-to-end perspective within the website, like, I do see the benefit there to get us more traffic.

Like, would it be, because I know, like, when they go to our resource center, they could choose, like, blog, white paper, whatever article, like, is this, like, is the glossary page an entirely new page on its own?

It a fairly new thing, so people would.

And one thing about glossary, I think, like, they're not exactly for your persona, because when you're talking about what is a DCS, like, your persona already knows what is a DCS.
So there is, like, there is a, like, a slight con, you would, of course, the goal here is grow the audience, like, the brand awareness for Imubit.
And that's why it wouldn't produce, like, 300 pages.
We have our SEOW seven articles a week.
We would just create this new page.

But, and then, yeah, it would just be, it's just a high level.
Leverage thing that we can do at a high volume, so we'll never really run out of an opportunity for this one, but this is perfect.
Thank you, Mariana, for sharing that.
I shared one page that is not worth.
Sorry.
Cool.
Maybe there's something on there, Another pro of having a structure like this is building contextual terms around it.
Yeah.
So every time we link to this, so they know, we signal that we are relevant in the industrial AI spaces, having this.

Well, my question about this particular framing, as you see, like you've got the compliance and then you've got the what is, is this pulling then in the AI play as well, because it's answering specific questions?
Yes, yes, definitely.
It definitely plays a significant role.
And AI mentions, but also we would add something related to Imubit the same way we do with articles.
So explain and how it connects.
Of course, we would only be, we would be prioritizing terms that have a lot of volume, that are good SEO opportunities, and also are closely related to Imubit solutions.
So, for example, stuff related to automation, that's not, not that it is connected, but it's more, a little more distant.
Yeah.

I don't, I don't have a problem with the strategy, and I think it's, you know, a little bit of just aligning on what terms we would use, and again, waiting to figure out whether, what we do on the CMS side.
Because again, I don't want to, I don't want to add a bunch of pages right now, especially this kind of volume, while we're trying to, to get it ported over.
I'm expecting a, an email today or tomorrow.
From the people that we're working with on whether or not we're going to do this.
So I should have an answer, at least, of what we want possibly not to do.
Yeah, I just want to echo, I'm just trying to echo, like, Cara's point, like, we are going to be going down the path very soon.
But no, thank you.
I do appreciate the value of this for, like, an SEO and indexing point of view.
I think that would help.
It makes perfect sense to me.
So I would like to do that, provided it fits in with our upcoming structure, and it does not impact the user experience.
It doesn't look, like, well, based on what you've shown.
It's, like, they'd have to look for it if they really wanted to find it.

Well, and the only other thing I want to say about that is, on the user experience side, kind of like the abnormal one you showed, is I want to make sure that if somebody does land there, somebody's searching, you know.
you're for for it, it, it, if

AI, and they land there because they were interested in that term, that they are getting to the relevant content, and it's not just, you know, that there's some linking out to the articles and the blogs and the, you know, the product stuff, so that they're not getting stuck there and being like, where did I end up?
Because then that's not as impactful as it could be.
Yeah, we, every, you know, every landing page or every blog post is an opportunity either to build trust or to break it.
And so we definitely don't want that experience of someone landing there and feeling like hoodwinked or feeling confused and frustrated.

So the information that they would need would be, and also like that's, I mean, thankfully, that's kind of the way that things are moving, especially with like AI answers of just the most helpful format will be what wins in a sense.
So thankfully, that makes our lives easier.
So yeah, just to answer that concern, definitely aligned there.
Marianne, I know you have
A point, and so I don't want to belabor this too much, but also, Tara, when you say difficult to manage, are you just thinking of the switch over to CMS, or are you thinking of updating it?
Because it should be relatively evergreen content, but I just want to make sure that I understand.
Yeah, I'm just being cognizant of the fact that, again, this could grow to a fairly large number.
And at the point that it grows through a fairly large number, mean, like you said, it should be fairly evergreen.
But, you know, as we're looking at moving to a new CMS, the things in the back of my mind are, what's the cost of change, right?
And what's the maintenance?
And, you know, what happens if we decide we need to change X, Y, or Z, right?
Like, just making sure that we're being cognizant of that.
Yeah, it's the same number of pages, nothing would change there.
So it would be like the same thing as we launching a new blog.
It's just a different format to include in the content strategy, basically.
And another pro I was thinking is that it gives us the opportunity to step into more like what is keywords.
If I'm creating like seven steps to a self-optimizing olefins plant, I won't say what is it.
I won't like approach these keywords, but they do have a lot of volume.
So if I tackle these keywords in a glossary term, I can like, what is a DCS?
So it's the total addressable market for volume.
Oh, no, I get it.
And again, I think we're all in agreement.
It's just the timing.
Just wanted to, just a reflection I wanted to bring for the future.
not something like...
it.
Okay, another thing I wanted to know, something that could be...
Really cool, I don't know if Imubit does that, so basically that's what I want to ask.
If it connects with feedstock prices or utility prices, can I actually, instead of having...
That's one of the inputs, right?
Like that's one of the levers that they're using to make these decisions, right?

If you think about all the players at the table and all the people that are inputting, definitely feedstock prices and that kind of thing, and utilities is some of the things they're looking to measure and to maintain.
So I could create, for example, a series of articles saying like five benefits of connecting or AI optimization with your...
Like considering adding the name of these categories, like of data input, for example, utility prices, feedstock prices.
Does this data come from a system or is it connected to UX?
be.
I mean, I would have to get a little more detail from the product team exactly how they're doing that.
But yes, theoretically, it's coming from a system and they're adding it in.
And this kind of ties a little bit more into where we're headed in terms of being able to take all of that input from the different areas.
And feedstock prices is one of those things, right?
Like that's a lever that they can pull, right?
Meaning like, oh, we need to, the feedstock price is going up.
We need to do something to bring our costs down.
Those are the types of levers that they are utilizing to make their decisions.
Yeah.
It's very connected.
For sure.
Yeah, I love, for example, one example here.
If propylene prices spike, the optimizer may shift cut points to make more propylene, even if that means slightly more energy use.
Right, because they're doing the trade-off, right?
Those are those little trade-offs that they have to make to figure out where this impacts their overall margin.
Yeah, this is actually an idea that Tiandra, the content manager that writes for your account, she came up with some ideas like, so scenarios I could simulate with Imubit.
So this would be a news, like a middle of funnel content we were discussing.
So if you can give us any information about these are the possibilities of scenarios you could simulate.
So I think this could also create good quality content for us.
And also I think this...
Stepping to more margin-related titles and keywords, like the difference of process optimization, actually business optimization, I think this could be also a way to differentiate the content.

Yeah, and I might have you hold on this particular one just until we start to get the messaging all together, because this one in particular really hits that kind of sweet spot of where we're headed in terms of bringing all of the stakeholders together to determine what we are calling coordinated operating strategies, right?

So that takes the economic element, so your planners, your economic buyer, that person, and adds in, oh, well, the plant still needs to run at an optimized safety level, like you've got to do all of these things over there.
It takes all of those people, defines that strategy, and then uses our system to build the model and do that with all of those things in mind.
And this one in particular is very, very tied to that concept.
Yeah, and depending on the persona, for example, a CFO will have KPIs related to more financial related data, and if I'm a process engineer, my KPIs will be more related to process optimization.

So I think once we have more clarity, like this comes up in the content, like the possibility to integrate this with feedstock data and utility prices, but it's a good content venue for us to explore in the future.
Yeah, it really, really is.
It's very closely tied to the idea of putting all of the stakeholders in there.
Finding all of the things that are important to them and creating the models around that to have those levers to pull, right?
For when feedstock prices change, that they can see if they pull this lever, how is that going to impact the energy, the yield, all of those different elements?
And that's what they will be able to do.
Maybe we could add this to the predictions content, like one of the predictions could be like being more integrated with and more useful for all stakeholders.
Yeah.
Yep.
Okay, this was great and this is all I had for today.
Do you have anything else?
I don't.
I'm starting to work on some, again, the content alignment, figuring out what we have, what we don't have.
I'm just from an internal perspective, starting to figure out what that means for middle of the funnel, bottom of the funnel type of content that we'll need to create.
We are, I would say, 75% of the way through the messaging, still got some big hurdles to go.
But once we have that, we'll get that over to you as well, just so that you guys are on the same page as, you know, kind of how we're trying to say things, what we want to say.
And I may have some more bottom of the funnel type of content to get over to you.
We had someone do a survey on workforce, and that data came back.
There was some initial work done on it, but I think we could take it and make it much more impactful.
So let me, I'll get, I'll get that over to you as soon as I have that ready, and then we can start talking.
I don't know if it's the same material, but we have a workforce research that was made, I guess, was like three months ago.
It may be the same.
On the conclusions, and I asked the team if they could send the charts and sheets because there wasn't the exact number I could.
I did the articles to be edited in the future for us to add more exact numbers.
Okay, yeah, I can get you the data.
I have the survey stuff, so I can get you the survey results.
I'll show you what we wrote based on that research, okay?
Yeah, please do.
I, unfortunately, have a hard stop.
I have another call I need to get to, but thank you guys for this.
Much appreciated.
Thank you for the time.
Bye, everyone.
Thanks, Mariana.
Bye, everyone.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Imubit and GrowthX to discuss content strategy, SEO performance, and future initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=100.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [20% increase in impressions, clicks, and sessions; AI mentions up from 30% to 35% presence](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=107.0)
  - [Glossary pages proposed as future SEO strategy, pending CMS decision and implementation](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=518.0)
  - [Content opportunities identified around integrating feedstock/utility prices with AI optimization](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1253.0)
  - [Imubit working on messaging alignment and new survey data for potential content creation](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1604.0)

## Topics

### SEO Performance Update

  - [20% increase in impressions, clicks, engaged sessions, and overall sessions](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=107.0)
  - [AI mentions increased from 30% to 35% presence in monitored prompts](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=138.0)
  - [SEMrush data shows good movement in top 3 to first page rankings (up to position 10)](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=178.0)
  - [Current focus on step-by-step guides to self-optimizing plants, incorporating advisory mode](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=198.0)

### Glossary Pages Strategy

  - [Proposed as an SEO play to leverage high-volume keywords not warranting full articles](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=518.0)
  - [Each term would have its own page, focusing on frequently used industry terms](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)
  - [Benefits: internal linking opportunities, contextual relevance for industrial AI](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=917.0)
  - [Concerns raised about potential volume (hundreds of terms) and user experience](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=678.0)
  - [Implementation to be considered after CMS decision to avoid migration issues](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)

### Content Opportunities with Feedstock/Utility Prices

  - [Potential for articles on benefits of connecting AI optimization with feedstock/utility price data](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1253.0)
  - [Example: "If propylene prices spike, the optimizer may shift cut points to make more propylene"](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1381.0)
  - [Aligns with Imubit's direction towards coordinated operating strategies](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1457.0)
  - [Opportunity to differentiate content by focusing on business optimization, not just process optimization](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)

### Future Content Directions

  - [Exploring scenarios that could be simulated with Imubit for middle-of-funnel content](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1405.0)
  - [Waiting for new messaging alignment before proceeding with certain topics](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1457.0)
  - [Potential to integrate stakeholder-specific KPIs (e.g., CFO vs. process engineer) in future content](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1520.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Await CMS decision before implementing glossary pages strategy](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)
  - [Imubit to provide updated messaging and survey data on workforce research](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1604.0)
  - [GrowthX to review existing workforce research content for potential updates](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1682.0)
  - [Explore content opportunities around coordinated operating strategies and stakeholder integration](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1457.0)
  - [Consider adding stakeholder integration to predictions content](https://fathom.video/share/FKAMBatvu3x5yQaNJ7PD5MjX9iwyZsZ2?tab=summary&timestamp=1586.0)

action_items.description: Send workforce survey results to Mariana for content creation
Show Tara articles written based on workforce research
